$schema_note: Format: { suppressions: [{ id, modules[], reason, expires(YYYY-MM-DD), references[]?, accept_reachable? }] }. Consumed by scripts/govulncheck.py. Each entry MUST document why the advisory is not exploitable in our usage and MUST set an expires date so suppressions are revisited. Reachable findings additionally require accept_reachable: true and a tracking issue in references.

suppressions.reason: Server-side AuthZ-plugin bypass in moby/moby (GHSA-x744-4wpc-v9h2). gokit's workload module only uses the docker *client* API to manage local containers; it does not run a Docker daemon, does not configure AuthZ plugins, and does not expose a network-reachable Docker socket. The vulnerable code path is in dockerd's request handling (server side), not in the client library we link. govulncheck flags client-side helpers as reachable because moby/moby is a hybrid client+daemon module — symbol-level analysis cannot tell the two halves apart. No upstream fix is available (Fixed in: N/A); migration to moby/moby/client tracked in #112.

Server-side off-by-one in moby plugin-privilege validation (GHSA-pxq6-2prw-chj9). gokit does not run a Docker daemon and does not invoke plugin-install APIs from the client. The vulnerable path is dockerd-side. Same hybrid-module false-positive pattern as GO-2026-4887. No upstream fix is available (Fixed in: N/A); migration to moby/moby/client tracked in #112.
